element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns fundamental swing mechanics on initial holes
Primary obstacle type across all courses in series
Added terrain complexity requiring shot planning around obstacles
Sand traps introduce penalty shots and recovery mechanics
Advanced technique for navigating complex course layouts
Optional creative tool for course customization

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully sinking putt (described as having 'amazing realistic' success sound)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based decision-making with variable shot difficulty; tension builds through course progression as obstacles accumulate

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Course editor and expansion disks provide optional customization

difficulty_curve.early_game: Four courses of varying difficulty introduce core mechanics; water hazards as primary obstacle
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Executive Edition added trees and bunkers, increasing spatial complexity and shot planning requirements
difficulty_curve.late_game: World Class Leader Board represents peak difficulty with all obstacle types plus punch shot technique requirement
notable_spikes: Introduction of trees (Executive Edition) - spatial complexity spike
Introduction of bunkers (Executive Edition) - penalty/recovery mechanic spike
Fictional Gauntlet Country Club (World Class) - designed as challenge course

notable_level_patterns.description: Four water-based courses in original Leader Board establish core obstacle type; water hazards remain primary hazard type throughout series
World Class Leader Board mixes realistic courses (Cypress Creek, Doral Country Club, St Andrews) with designed challenge course (Gauntlet Country Club)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World Class Leader Board - four courses

notable_level_patterns.description: Leaderboard Tournament provided four-course expansion disks; World Class had three expansion disks, allowing extended play
